title: pittosporum perryanum var. perryanum
intro: Pittosporum perryanum var. perryanum, also known as the Northern or Kohuhu, is a small tree or shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive, fragrant flowers and dark green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with extracts believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pittosporum perryanum var. perryanum include its potential to soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, or used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
